stem: In the xy-plane, the graph of a function crosses the x-axis at the point (−7, 0) and the y-axis at the point (0, 14). What is the x-intercept of the graph?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The x-intercept is the point at which a graph crosses the x-axis (where y = 0). The graph crosses the x-axis at (−7, 0).

Choice B has the wrong sign. Choice C swaps the coordinates. Choice D is the y-intercept.
